ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham went and spoke to them truthfully, saying, 'The Lord saw the great things He did, fulfilling His great promises. He led them out to the land flowing with milk and honey.'

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: They went to the market, but Jacob stayed in the corner of the house, and they took the other side of the market, and he was left alone in the corner of the house.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.

translated: If Unimbɔti comes, you also come as Unimbɔti comes. You are the one who takes the impu.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: Even the ant has its place in the forest, even the great elephant has its place in the forest, even the smallest creature has its place in the forest, therefore, everyone has their role to play.

translated: Abraham took his family and belongings, and went to the land of the Canaanites, because of the great famine, and he traveled to Egypt, where there were many people.

translated: Yesterday, I saw that the day before yesterday was very early, and Jacob came to ask me to go with him to the place where we will meet, so that we can gather the items, and today we will go to that place to collect them.
